Lets try MobileNet instead of a custom CNN - Devendra¶

Prompts

  • which is better custom CNN or a pre-trained model like MobileNetV2
  • how do I fine-tune MobileNetV2 for my dataset
  • now plot a ROC AUC curve and confusion matrix

Steps¶

Lets Load and preprocesses the data

Select 20,000 random images

Lets Build a CNN model (based on MobileNetV2 for efficiency)

Train and evaluates the model

In [9]:
import os
import json
import random
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator
from tensorflow.keras.applications import MobileNetV2
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense, Flatten, GlobalAveragePooling2D
from tensorflow.keras.models import Model

# Paths
PHOTOS_FOLDER = "/Users/DSS7/Downloads/Yelp Photos/yelp_photos/photos"
JSON_FILE = "/Users/DSS7/Downloads/Yelp Photos/yelp_photos/photos.json"

# Load JSON data
with open(JSON_FILE, 'r') as f:
    data = [json.loads(line) for line in f]

# Convert JSON data to a DataFrame
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Select 20,000 random samples
df_sampled = df.sample(n=20000, random_state=42).reset_index(drop=True)

# Generate file paths
df_sampled["filename"] = df_sampled["photo_id"].apply(lambda x: os.path.join(PHOTOS_FOLDER, x + ".jpg"))

# Ensure only valid image files exist
df_sampled = df_sampled[df_sampled["filename"].apply(os.path.exists)]

# Get unique class labels
unique_labels = df_sampled["label"].unique()
NUM_CLASSES = len(unique_labels)

# Encode labels as strings
label_to_index = {label: str(idx) for idx, label in enumerate(unique_labels)}  # Convert indices to strings
df_sampled["class"] = df_sampled["label"].map(label_to_index)

# Split data into train (80%) and validation (20%)
train_df = df_sampled.sample(frac=0.8, random_state=42)
val_df = df_sampled.drop(train_df.index)

# Image parameters
IMG_SIZE = (224, 224)
BATCH_SIZE = 32

# Image data generator
datagen = ImageDataGenerator(rescale=1.0/255, validation_split=0.2)

train_generator = datagen.flow_from_dataframe(
    dataframe=train_df,
    x_col="filename",
    y_col="class",
    target_size=IMG_SIZE,
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    class_mode="categorical"
)

val_generator = datagen.flow_from_dataframe(
    dataframe=val_df,
    x_col="filename",
    y_col="class",
    target_size=IMG_SIZE,
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    class_mode="categorical"
)

# Load MobileNetV2 model (pre-trained)
base_model = MobileNetV2(weights="imagenet", include_top=False, input_shape=(224, 224, 3))
base_model.trainable = False  # Freeze base model

# Add new classification layers
x = base_model.output
x = GlobalAveragePooling2D()(x)
x = Dense(128, activation="relu")(x)
out = Dense(NUM_CLASSES, activation="softmax")(x)  # Ensure correct number of classes

# Create model
model = Model(inputs=base_model.input, outputs=out)

# Compile model
model.compile(optimizer="adam", loss="categorical_crossentropy", metrics=["accuracy"])

# Train model
model.fit(train_generator, validation_data=val_generator, epochs=10)

# Save model
model.save("yelp_cnn_model.h5")

print("✅ Model training complete. Saved as 'yelp_cnn_model.h5'")

Found 15995 validated image filenames belonging to 5 classes.
Found 3999 validated image filenames belonging to 5 classes.
Epoch 1/10

Do not pass these arguments to `fit()`, as they will be ignored.

WARNING:absl:You are saving your model as an HDF5 file via `model.save()` or `keras.saving.save_model(model)`. This file format is considered legacy. We recommend using instead the native Keras format, e.g. `model.save('my_model.keras')` or `keras.saving.save_model(model, 'my_model.keras')`.

Lets make predictions¶

Loads the trained yelp_cnn_model.h5.

Reads an image, resizes it to 224x224, and normalizes pixel values.

Predicts the image class using the trained model.

Displays the image with the predicted label.

In [31]:
import numpy as np
import tensorflow as tf
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing import image

# Load the trained model without compiling (fixes warning)
model = tf.keras.models.load_model("yelp_cnn_model.h5", compile=False)

# Ensure label mapping is correct
unique_labels = list(label_to_index.keys())  # Get unique labels from dataset
index_to_label = {idx: label for idx, label in enumerate(unique_labels)}

# Function to preprocess and predict multiple images
def predict_images(image_paths, model, index_to_label):
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(15, 10))  # 2 rows, 3 columns
    axes = axes.flatten()

    for i, img_path in enumerate(image_paths):
        img = image.load_img(img_path, target_size=(224, 224))
        img_array = image.img_to_array(img)
        img_array = np.expand_dims(img_array, axis=0) / 255.0  # Normalize
        
        # Make prediction
        prediction = model.predict(img_array)
        predicted_class = np.argmax(prediction, axis=1)[0]

        # Get predicted label
        predicted_label = index_to_label.get(predicted_class, "Unknown")
        
        # Display image with label
        axes[i].imshow(img)
        axes[i].set_title(f"Predicted: {predicted_label}", fontsize=12)
        axes[i].axis("off")

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# Example image paths (replace with actual paths)
image_paths = [
    "/Users/devendra/Desktop/NLP/Yelp Photos/yelp_photos/photos/__02nEL2xViYvZihvV4_hw.jpg",
    "/Users/devendra/Desktop/NLP/Yelp Photos/yelp_photos/photos/__smnLylDOFbWm4SBWXDjA.jpg",
    "/Users/devendra/Desktop/NLP/Yelp Photos/yelp_photos/photos/_2Bd4KGDBWW2XzPwbpGoaw.jpg",
    "/Users/devendra/Desktop/NLP/Yelp Photos/yelp_photos/photos/_-CniMIJfzpM_7Lb66AAmQ.jpg",
    "/Users/devendra/Desktop/NLP/Yelp Photos/yelp_photos/photos/_-jExCc_qvHX_FqbhW6-iw.jpg",
    "/Users/devendra/Desktop/NLP/Yelp Photos/yelp_photos/photos/_1y-cgkoBn7RpCNkUBOeKw.jpg"
]

predict_images(image_paths, model, index_to_label)

Lets compute confusion matrix and AUC¶

In [49]:
import numpy as np
import tensorflow as tf
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, classification_report, roc_curve, auc
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator

# Load the trained model
model = tf.keras.models.load_model("yelp_cnn_model.h5", compile=False)

# Ensure label mapping is correct
index_to_label = {idx: label for label, idx in label_to_index.items()}  # Reverse mapping

# Get class names
class_names = list(label_to_index.keys())

# Get predictions on validation data
y_true = []
y_pred_probs = []

for _ in range(len(val_generator)):
    X_batch, y_batch = next(val_generator)  # Use `next()` instead of `.next()`
    y_true.extend(np.argmax(y_batch, axis=1))  # Convert one-hot to class indices
    y_pred_probs.extend(model.predict(X_batch))  # Get predicted probabilities

# Convert to NumPy arrays
y_true = np.array(y_true)
y_pred_probs = np.array(y_pred_probs)
y_pred = np.argmax(y_pred_probs, axis=1)  # Get predicted class indices

# 1. Confusion Matrix
cm = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred)
print(cm)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt="d", cmap="Blues", xticklabels=class_names, yticklabels=class_names, annot_kws={"size": 14})
plt.xlabel("Predicted Label", fontsize=14)
plt.ylabel("True Label", fontsize=14)
plt.title("Confusion Matrix", fontsize=16)
plt.xticks(fontsize=12)
plt.yticks(fontsize=12)
plt.show()

# 2. Classification Report
print("Classification Report:\n", classification_report(y_true, y_pred, target_names=class_names))

# 3. AUC-ROC Curve (One-vs-Rest)
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))

for i in range(len(class_names)):
    fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_true == i, y_pred_probs[:, i])
    roc_auc = auc(fpr, tpr)
    plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"{class_names[i]} (AUC = {roc_auc:.2f})")

plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", label="Random Guess")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate", fontsize=14)
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate", fontsize=14)
plt.title("AUC-ROC Curve", fontsize=16)
plt.legend(loc="lower right", fontsize=12)
plt.show()
